skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Kling 3.0 video generation on RunComfy. Kling 3.0 (also called Kling V3.0) is Kuaishou Technology's third-generation multi-shot video model with native synchronized audio and consistent character identity across shots. This skill covers all six Kling 3.0 endpoints, spanning three rendering tiers (Standard, Pro, 4K) and two modes (text-to-video, image-to-video). Calls runcomfy run kling/kling-3.0/<tier>/<mode> through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "kling", "kling 3.0", "kling v3", "kling pro", "kling 4k", "kling text to video", "kling image to video", or any explicit ask to generate or animate with Kling 3.0.
skill.summary: Generate cinematic video clips with Kling 3.0 AI through RunComfy CLI. Supports text-to-video and image-to-video across Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 99 potential issues, but evaluation determined 95+ are false positives. The scanner detected Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution, path traversal placeholders in example commands, and misinterpreted keywords as crypto issues. No command injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious patterns exist. All network access is limited to the RunComfy platform API endpoints documented in the skill. Token storage follows standard OAuth practices. The skill is safe for publication.

medium_findings.title: CLI External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes the runcomfy CLI via shell commands. While no user input injection was found, the pattern of CLI invocation warrants documentation as a standard external-commands risk factor.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs
low_findings.description: The skill documents RunComfy API endpoints as hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate third-party service endpoints required for the CLI integration. No credential exfiltration or unexpected network destinations detected.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Placeholder Detection (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged <absolute/path> placeholders in example commands as path traversal. These are documented user-provided output directories, not exploitable vulnerabilities.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Reference (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json as hidden file access. This is standard OAuth token storage documented as a security feature, not a vulnerability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Fence Misinterpretation
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner interpreted Markdown triple-backtick fences (```) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is a false positive - the skill is documentation-only with no executable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Keyword Misinterpretation as Crypto
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 'API', 'AES', and other keywords as weak cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic code exists in this documentation file.

content.user_title: Generate cinematic videos with Kling 3.0 AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional video content requires expensive production equipment and expertise. Kling 3.0 on RunComfy enables AI-powered cinematic video generation with text-to-video and image-to-video capabilities across Standard, Pro, and 4K quality tiers.
actual_capabilities: Generate up to 15-second cinematic video clips from text prompts
Animate static images into video with consistent character identity
Produce multi-shot sequences with synchronized audio in one generation
Choose from three quality tiers: Standard (1080p), Pro (1080p), and 4K (3840x2160)
Generate synchronized native audio including dialogue, ambient sound, and music
limitations: Maximum 15 seconds per generation; longer videos require stitching multiple clips
Image-to-video mode requires publicly accessible HTTPS image URLs
4K tier is approximately 5x the cost of Standard tier

use_cases.title: Marketing and brand content creators
use_cases.description: Produce professional 4K brand films, social media shorts, and A/B test variants without video production equipment or crew.

use_cases.title: Creative professionals and animators
use_cases.description: Animate character art, product photos, and concept illustrations into polished video clips with multi-shot sequences.

use_cases.title: Developers building video pipelines
use_cases.description: Integrate AI video generation into automated content workflows through the RunComfy CLI for programmatic video production.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic video of [describe your scene, subject, and action]. Use [camera movement type like wide shot or tracking shot]. Include [atmosphere and lighting details].
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate the image at [image URL] with [describe motion: camera movement, subject action, environmental changes]. Preserve [identity elements to maintain]. Style: [artistic direction].
prompt_templates.scenario: Animate a still image into a moving video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-shot cinematic sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-shot cinematic sequence: Shot 1: [wide establishing shot description]. Shot 2: [medium action description]. Shot 3: [close-up detail]. Include [audio description: ambient sound, music style, dialogue]. Quality: [Standard/Pro/4K].
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional multi-shot video with scene transitions

prompt_templates.title: Vertical social media content
prompt_templates.prompt: 9:16 vertical format. [Describe subject and action in close-up]. Style: [documentary/cinematic/commercial]. Ambient audio: [describe soundscape]. For [social platform target].
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimized vertical video for social platforms

output_examples.input: A barista in a black apron pulls a single espresso shot, steam rising into morning sun, rich crema slowly forming. Close-up handheld, shallow depth of field, warm cafe ambience.
output_examples.output: A 5-second 16:9 cinematic clip showing the espresso extraction process with warm lighting and shallow depth of field. The steam rises naturally, crema forms slowly, and ambient cafe sounds enhance the atmosphere.

output_examples.input: Multi-shot: Shot 1: wide of city skyline at golden hour. Shot 2: medium two-shot of couple toasting. Shot 3: tight on woman's smile, soft bokeh. With ambient string music.
output_examples.output: A 10-second Pro-tier video with three distinct camera movements. Scene transitions are smooth, lighting remains consistent, and the couple's identity is preserved across all shots. Background music adds emotional depth.

output_examples.input: Animate [product photo URL] with gentle camera dolly-in, subtle breathing motion, identity-stable features, soft natural light.
output_examples.output: An 8-second 1080p video smoothly animating the product from stillness. The camera push-in is subtle, lighting remains natural, and product details stay sharp. Ready for e-commerce use.

best_practices: Lead prompts with camera and motion language (wide shot, tracking, dolly-in) for best framing results
Use numbered shots (Shot 1, Shot 2) for multi-shot sequences to ensure consistent identity across scenes
Start with Standard tier for iteration and testing before committing to Pro or 4K for final outputs
anti_patterns: Avoid conflicting style cues in a single prompt; pick one or two style anchors for consistent results
Do not request more than 15 seconds in one call; segment longer narratives into multiple generations
Avoid unsupported aspect ratios; use only the six documented ratios to prevent cropping or errors

faq.question: What is the maximum duration for a Kling 3.0 video?
faq.answer: Each Kling 3.0 generation supports 3 to 15 seconds. For longer narratives, segment your script into multiple calls and stitch the clips together.

faq.question: How does Kling V3.0 4K pricing compare to other tiers?
faq.answer: Standard costs $0.084 per second, Pro costs $0.112 per second, and 4K is a flat $0.42 per second. The 4K tier is approximately 5x the cost of Standard.

faq.question: Can Kling 3.0 generate audio with video?
faq.answer: Yes. Set generate_audio to true. Kling 3.0 produces synchronized dialogue, ambient sounds, and music in one pass. Audio adds about 50% cost on Standard and Pro; the 4K tier charges flat rate with audio.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Kling 3.0 support?
faq.answer: Supported aspect ratios are 16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4, and 21:9. The 4K tier renders 21:9 as wide cinema crops at native 3840x2160 resolution.

faq.question: What are the quality differences between Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers?
faq.answer: Standard offers up to 1080p for fast iteration. Pro provides highest 1080p fidelity with better motion realism. 4K delivers native 3840x2160 output for finished masters and big-screen content.

faq.question: Does Kling 3.0 support multi-shot sequences in one generation?
faq.answer: Yes. All tiers accept numbered multi-segment prompts (Shot 1, Shot 2, etc.). Kling 3.0 preserves character identity across shots within a single generation.
